title: Adopt code of conduct and moderation process
summary: Create enforceable participation, reporting, moderation, appeal, privacy and conflict-of-interest rules for technical and community spaces.

title: Publish contributor onboarding, DCO, and clean-room disclosure flow
summary: Enable lawful, secure, architecture-aligned contributions with explicit source provenance and proprietary-exposure handling.

title: Publish evidence-based public roadmap and support labels
summary: Expose funded/owned work, hypotheses, gates, target support, limitations and stop decisions without promising unsupported phone features.

summary: Publish searchable linked specifications, glossary, prior art, tasks, evidence and onboarding while keeping Git source authoritative.

title: Create contributor starter tasks and learning paths
summary: Turn architecture, docs, tests, emulation and hardware evidence into safe bounded entry points by skill level.

title: Run prioritized technical and vendor outreach campaign
summary: Contact capability experts, board/silicon vendors, open-phone communities, camera/modem vendors, labs and funding programs with precise asks.

title: Create grant and sponsor pipeline
summary: Identify aligned public-interest, research, hardware and commercial funding with transparent scope, IP/governance and reporting cost.

title: Adopt sponsorship and commercial-conflict policy
summary: Define disclosure, recusal, technical independence, branding, data, exclusivity and public-reporting rules for sponsors and partners.

title: Launch bounded bounty program after review capacity exists
summary: Fund well-specified tests, drivers, tooling, documentation and experiments without incentivizing unsafe or legally uncertain work.

title: Establish hardware maintainer and CI specimen program
summary: Assign target owners, support levels, specimens, revisions, lab access, release tests, security/update responsibility and succession.

title: Run external community hardware-kit reproduction pilot
summary: Have independent maintainers build, flash, boot, test, update and recover the selected kit from public documentation and tools.

title: Establish translation and accessibility contribution workflow
summary: Enable reviewed translations, terminology, pseudolocalization fixes, accessibility audits and assistive-technology contributions with safe release ownership.

title: Prepare evidence-backed public project launch
summary: Publish the cleared brand, mission, architecture, limitations, governance, licenses, security contact, roadmap, starter paths and reproducible first artifact.
